selected_plan.title: Deliver the first async interview practice MVP
selected_plan.summary: Build a small browser-based experience for session setup, answer capture, rubric feedback, and saved results.

retained_alternatives.title: Chat-only prototype
retained_alternatives.summary: Use a single conversational surface with minimal structure and looser reports.

leaf_tasks.title: Define session and feedback domain contracts
leaf_tasks.description: Model the interview session, question sequence, saved result, and rubric summary structures.
leaf_tasks.why: The rest of the MVP depends on having stable session and feedback shapes.
done_criteria: Session and summary data structures are defined
The storage shape supports saved history

verification.description: Review that session and summary structures support the selected approach.

leaf_tasks.title: Implement the browser session flow
leaf_tasks.description: Create the user flow for role selection, question progression, and answer capture.
leaf_tasks.why: The MVP needs a complete path from session start to completed answers.
done_criteria: A user can start and complete a short session
Answers are captured in the selected session structure

verification.description: Walk through the session flow in the browser.

leaf_tasks.title: Implement feedback generation and saved session history
leaf_tasks.description: Generate a rubric summary for a completed session and make saved reports reviewable later.
leaf_tasks.why: Feedback and recall are the main proof of value for the MVP.
done_criteria: Completed sessions produce saved feedback summaries
Past session summaries can be revisited

verification.description: Complete a session and confirm the saved summary can be reopened.

risk_summary.description: The rubric evaluator may produce weak feedback without tighter structure.
risk_summary.mitigation: Use explicit rubric templates and sample-session testing.

risk_summary.description: Too much setup may reduce first-session completion.
risk_summary.mitigation: Keep the setup flow short and template-driven.
